rules.title: Potential SSN detected
rules.description: A pattern matching Social Security Number format was found in the code.
rules.recommendation: Remove hardcoded SSN. Use secure storage and encryption for sensitive identifiers.

rules.title: Medical Record Number exposure
rules.description: A hardcoded medical record number was detected.
rules.recommendation: Never hardcode MRNs. Fetch from secure, encrypted storage.

rules.title: Date of birth exposure
rules.description: Date of birth information may be hardcoded or improperly handled.
rules.recommendation: Encrypt DOB at rest and in transit. Apply minimum necessary principle.

rules.title: Diagnosis code in source
rules.description: ICD-10 diagnosis codes found in source code.
rules.recommendation: Load diagnosis codes from secure configuration, not source code.

rules.title: PHI identifier in URL pattern
rules.description: URL pattern suggests PHI may be exposed in URLs.
rules.recommendation: Never include PHI in URLs. Use opaque tokens or encrypted identifiers.

rules.title: Patient name in console output
rules.description: Patient names may be logged to console, exposing PHI.
rules.recommendation: Remove patient identifiers from logs. Use anonymized IDs for debugging.

rules.title: PHI data in console output
rules.description: Sensitive health information may be logged to console.
rules.recommendation: Never log PHI to console. Use structured logging with PHI redaction.

rules.title: Patient object serialized to console
rules.description: Patient objects are being serialized and logged, potentially exposing all PHI fields.
rules.recommendation: Create a sanitized version of patient objects for logging, excluding PHI fields.

rules.title: PHI in template literal log
rules.description: Template literal logging may expose PHI data.
rules.recommendation: Avoid interpolating PHI into log messages.

rules.title: PHI stored in localStorage
rules.description: PHI data is being stored in localStorage which is not encrypted and persists indefinitely.
rules.recommendation: Never store PHI in localStorage. Use encrypted server-side storage with proper access controls.

rules.title: PHI stored in sessionStorage
rules.description: PHI data is being stored in sessionStorage which is not encrypted.
rules.recommendation: Avoid storing PHI in browser storage. Use secure, encrypted server-side sessions.

rules.title: PHI stored in cookies
rules.description: PHI data may be stored in browser cookies without encryption.
rules.recommendation: Never store PHI in cookies. Use encrypted server-side sessions with secure, httpOnly cookies for session IDs only.

rules.title: PHI potentially stored in IndexedDB
rules.description: PHI may be stored in IndexedDB which lacks built-in encryption.
rules.recommendation: If using IndexedDB for PHI, implement client-side encryption and proper key management.

rules.title: Patient email handling detected
rules.description: Code handles patient email addresses which are PHI.
rules.recommendation: Ensure patient emails are encrypted and access is logged.

rules.title: Patient phone handling detected
rules.description: Code handles patient phone numbers which are PHI.
rules.recommendation: Ensure patient contact info is encrypted and access is logged.

rules.title: Patient address handling detected
rules.description: Code handles patient addresses which are PHI.
rules.recommendation: Ensure patient addresses are encrypted and access is logged.

rules.title: PHI in URL query parameter
rules.description: PHI identifiers are being passed as URL query parameters, which may be logged in server logs, browser history, and referrer headers.
rules.recommendation: Never pass PHI in URLs. Use POST requests with encrypted body or session-based lookups.

rules.title: PHI interpolated into URL
rules.description: PHI values are being interpolated into URLs, exposing sensitive data.
rules.recommendation: Use opaque tokens or encrypted references instead of PHI in URLs.

rules.title: PHI in fetch URL
rules.description: Fetch request URL contains PHI identifiers.
rules.recommendation: Pass PHI in request body with proper encryption, not in URLs.

rules.title: PHI in HTTP header
rules.description: PHI data is being set in HTTP headers, which may be logged or cached.
rules.recommendation: Never transmit PHI in HTTP headers. Use encrypted request/response body.

rules.title: PHI in headers object
rules.description: Headers object contains PHI-related fields.
rules.recommendation: Remove PHI from headers. Transmit sensitive data in encrypted request body only.

rules.title: Patient data in authorization context
rules.description: Patient identifiers may be exposed in authorization headers.
rules.recommendation: Use opaque session tokens for authorization, not patient identifiers.

rules.title: PHI in email content
rules.description: Patient data may be included in email body, which is typically unencrypted.
rules.recommendation: Avoid sending PHI via email. Use secure patient portals with authentication.

rules.title: PHI in email template
rules.description: Email templates may contain patient data placeholders.
rules.recommendation: Do not include PHI in email templates. Send secure links to authenticated portals instead.

rules.title: Potential PHI in email subject
rules.description: Email subject lines may contain patient-related information.
rules.recommendation: Keep email subjects generic. Never include patient names, diagnoses, or identifiers.

rules.title: PHI logged without redaction
rules.description: Patient data is being logged without apparent redaction or masking.
rules.recommendation: Implement PHI redaction in logging. Use structured logging with automatic PII masking.

rules.title: PHI written to log file
rules.description: Patient data may be written directly to log files.
rules.recommendation: Use structured logging with PHI redaction. Never write raw PHI to log files.

rules.title: Debug mode with patient data
rules.description: Debug mode enabled in code handling patient data may expose PHI.
rules.recommendation: Ensure debug logging excludes PHI or uses proper redaction.

rules.title: Hardcoded SSN in skill prompt
rules.description: Social Security Number found in skill definition
rules.recommendation: Never hardcode PHI in skill prompts. Use placeholders like {{patient_id}} instead.

rules.title: Patient name in example
rules.description: Real patient name appears in skill prompt example
rules.recommendation: Use fictional names (e.g., "John Doe") or template variables {{patient_name}}

rules.title: Date of birth in prompt
rules.description: Date of birth found in skill definition
rules.recommendation: Use template variable {{date_of_birth}} instead of actual dates

rules.title: Medical Record Number exposed
rules.description: Medical Record Number found in skill prompt
rules.recommendation: Never include real MRNs. Use {{medical_record_number}} placeholder.

rules.title: Diagnosis code in prompt
rules.description: ICD diagnosis code found in skill example
rules.recommendation: Use generic examples or template variables for diagnoses

rules.title: API key in skill configuration
rules.description: Hardcoded API key found in skill
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY} or prompt user for keys

rules.title: AWS credentials exposed
rules.description: AWS credentials found in skill definition
rules.recommendation: Never hardcode AWS credentials. Use IAM roles or environment variables.

rules.title: Database password in connection string
rules.description: Database password exposed in connection string
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables: ${DB_PASSWORD} or credential manager

rules.title: Bearer token hardcoded
rules.description: Bearer authentication token found in skill
rules.recommendation: Tokens should be fetched securely at runtime, not hardcoded

rules.title: Private key in skill
rules.description: Private cryptographic key found in skill definition
rules.recommendation: Never include private keys. Use key management service.

rules.title: Data exfiltration detected
rules.description: Potential data exfiltration via curl to external domain
rules.recommendation: Review external API calls. Healthcare data should not be sent to unknown endpoints.

rules.title: Reverse shell attempt
rules.description: Reverse shell command detected - potential backdoor
rules.recommendation: REJECT THIS SKILL. This is a clear malicious pattern.

rules.title: Atomic Stealer pattern
rules.description: Pattern matches known Atomic Stealer malware distribution
rules.recommendation: REJECT THIS SKILL. This matches malware signatures from Snyk analysis.

rules.title: Credential scraping detected
rules.description: Commands that search for credential files
rules.recommendation: Verify legitimacy. Skills should not scrape credential files.

rules.title: Obfuscated command execution
rules.description: Command substitution or eval - common in malware
rules.recommendation: Review carefully. Obfuscation often hides malicious intent.

rules.title: PHI transmitted over HTTP
rules.description: Skill transmits PHI over unencrypted HTTP
rules.recommendation: Use HTTPS for all PHI transmission. HTTP violates HIPAA encryption requirements.

rules.title: PHI access without audit logging
rules.description: Skill accesses PHI database without audit logging
rules.recommendation: Add audit logging: auditLog.record({ action, userId, resourceId })

rules.title: PHI logged to console/files
rules.description: Skill logs PHI to console or log files
rules.recommendation: Never log PHI. Use redacted logging: logger.info({ patientId: "***" })

rules.title: PHI in URL parameters
rules.description: Skill passes PHI in URL query parameters
rules.recommendation: Use POST with encrypted body. URLs are logged by proxies/servers.

rules.title: Missing encryption for PHI storage
rules.description: Skill stores PHI in browser storage without encryption
rules.recommendation: Use encrypted storage or server-side session storage only.

rules.title: Missing Multi-Factor Authentication for PHI Access
rules.description: Endpoints accessing PHI must enforce MFA. All auth configs must require multi-factor authentication.

rules.title: ePHI Stored Without Encryption at Rest
rules.description: All ePHI must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or stronger.

rules.title: Missing Automatic Session Timeout
rules.description: PHI access sessions must auto-expire within 15 minutes of inactivity.

rules.title: Missing Immediate Access Revocation
rules.description: User deactivation must immediately invalidate all sessions and tokens.

rules.title: Missing Breach Notification Mechanism
rules.description: Must have automated breach detection and notification within 24 hours.

rules.title: Missing Network Segmentation for PHI
rules.description: PHI services must be network-segmented with restricted CORS and firewall rules.

rules.title: Generate ePHI Technology Asset Inventory
rules.description: Automatic inventory of all systems processing, storing, or transmitting ePHI.

rules.title: Generate ePHI Flow Map
rules.description: Automatic mapping of PHI data flow through system (input → processing → storage → output).

rules.title: Missing Vulnerability Scanning Configuration
rules.description: Must have automated vulnerability scanning (Dependabot, Snyk, Trivy) in CI/CD.

rules.title: Authentication Routes Without Rate Limiting
rules.description: Authentication route (/login, /signin, /auth, /register, /signup, /password-reset, /forgot-password) defined without rate limiting middleware (rateLimit, rateLimiter, throttle, slowDown, @upstash/ratelimit). Vulnerable to brute force attacks.
rules.recommendation: Add rate limiting middleware to authentication routes. Example: const limiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 5 }); app.post("/login", limiter, loginHandler). Prevent brute force attacks on auth endpoints.

rules.title: Open CORS Configuration (origin: "*")
rules.description: CORS configured with origin: "*" or Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *. PHI endpoints must restrict access to trusted domains only. Exposes API to cross-origin attacks from any domain.
rules.recommendation: Restrict CORS to trusted domains. Example: cors({ origin: ["https://app.example.com", "https://admin.example.com"] }) or use environment variables: cors({ origin: process.env.ALLOWED_ORIGINS.split(",") }). Never use origin: "*" for PHI endpoints.

rules.title: PHI Data in URL Query Parameters
rules.description: PHI data (ssn, dob, patient_name, patientName, diagnosis, medication, mrn, health_record) passed as URL query parameters (?ssn=, &dob=). URLs are logged by servers, proxies, and browsers, exposing PHI in logs.
rules.recommendation: Never pass PHI in URL query parameters. Use POST request body instead. Example: Instead of GET /api/patient?ssn=123-45-6789, use POST /api/patient with body: { ssn: "123-45-6789" }. URLs are logged in server logs, proxy logs, and browser history.

rules.title: Debug/Verbose Mode Without Environment Gate
rules.description: Debug or verbose mode enabled (debug:true, DEBUG:true, verbose:true, devTools:true) without NODE_ENV environment gate. May expose sensitive information in production.
rules.recommendation: Gate debug/verbose modes with environment checks. Example: debug: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development", or use environment variables: debug: process.env.DEBUG === "true". Never hardcode debug:true in production config.

rules.title: Web Server Without Security Headers Middleware
rules.description: Web server created (express(), createServer, new Hono, new Elysia) without helmet() or security headers middleware (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options). Missing security headers expose app to XSS, clickjacking, and MIME sniffing attacks.
rules.recommendation: Add helmet middleware for Express: app.use(helmet()), or set security headers manually: res.setHeader("X-Frame-Options", "DENY"); res.setHeader("X-Content-Type-Options", "nosniff"); res.setHeader("Content-Security-Policy", "default-src 'self'"). Use helmet for comprehensive security headers.

rules.title: Test Framework Imports in Production Code
rules.description: Test framework imports (jest, vitest, mocha, chai, faker, cypress) found in production code (non-test files). Test frameworks should only be imported in test files (*.test.*, *.spec.*).
rules.recommendation: Remove test framework imports from production code. Test frameworks should only be imported in test files (*.test.ts, *.spec.js, etc.). Move test utilities to separate test helper files.

rules.title: Authentication Configuration Without MFA Enabled
rules.description: Auth provider configuration (NextAuth, Clerk, Auth0, Supabase) does not have MFA/2FA/TOTP enabled
rules.recommendation: Enable MFA in your auth provider configuration. For NextAuth: add adapter with MFA support. For Clerk: enable MFA in dashboard. For Auth0: enable MFA in tenant settings. For Supabase: enable MFA in auth settings.

rules.title: Login Flow Without Second Factor Authentication
rules.description: Login/sign-in flow authenticates with only email and password without requiring second factor verification
rules.recommendation: Add second factor verification to login flow. After successful password authentication, require MFA token/TOTP code before granting access. Example: if (user.mfaEnabled && !mfaToken) return { error: "MFA required" }

rules.title: MFA Bypass Detected in Code
rules.description: Code explicitly bypasses, skips, or disables MFA requirements
rules.recommendation: Remove MFA bypass code. MFA should be mandatory for all users accessing PHI. If testing is needed, use proper test isolation instead of disabling MFA in production code.

rules.title: Unsanitized Error Details Sent to User
rules.description: Response sends error.stack or error.message directly to user without sanitization, potentially exposing sensitive system information
rules.recommendation: Never send error.stack or error.message directly to users. Use generic error messages for production. Example: res.status(500).json({ error: "An error occurred" }). Log detailed errors server-side only.

rules.title: PHI Data in Error Logs or Thrown Errors
rules.description: Protected Health Information (patient, ssn, dob, mrn, diagnosis, medication, healthRecord) exposed in console logs, logger, or thrown errors
rules.recommendation: Never log PHI in error messages. Redact sensitive data before logging. Example: logger.error("Error processing patient", { patientId: redact(patient.id) }). Use patient IDs only, never full PHI.

rules.title: Database Without Backup Configuration
rules.description: Database usage detected (supabase, prisma, mongoose, drizzle, typeorm, sequelize, knex) without backup/snapshot/replicate configuration references in project. Advisory: Full verification requires infrastructure inspection.
rules.recommendation: Configure automated database backups. Examples: Enable Supabase automatic backups, configure Prisma backup scripts, set up MongoDB Atlas continuous backups, or use pg_dump/mongodump in CI/CD. Document backup schedule and retention policy.

rules.title: PHI Records Created Without Retention Fields
rules.description: PHI record creation (insert/create/save/upsert on patient/health/medical/clinical tables) without retention fields (expiresAt, ttl, retainUntil, retentionPeriod, deleteAfter). HIPAA requires minimum necessary retention periods.
rules.recommendation: Add retention fields to PHI record creation. Example: { ...patientData, expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + 7 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000) } or { ...data, retentionPeriod: "7years", deleteAfter: calculateDeleteDate() }. Define retention policy based on record type and regulatory requirements.

rules.title: JSON Body Parser Without Size Limit
rules.description: express.json() or bodyParser.json() configured without limit option. Unlimited body size can lead to DoS attacks via large payload submissions.
rules.recommendation: Configure body size limits. Example: app.use(express.json({ limit: "10mb" })) or bodyParser.json({ limit: "1mb" }). Set limit based on expected payload size. Typical values: 1mb for APIs, 10mb for file uploads.

rules.title: Weak Password Hashing Algorithm Detected
rules.description: Using MD5, SHA1, or SHA256 for password hashing instead of secure algorithms like bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt
rules.recommendation: Use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt for password hashing. Example: await bcrypt.hash(password, 10) or await argon2.hash(password). Never use MD5, SHA1, or simple SHA256 for passwords.

rules.title: Hardcoded Credentials Detected
rules.description: Credentials (password, apiKey, secret, token, connectionString) hardcoded as string literals instead of using environment variables
rules.recommendation: Move credentials to environment variables. Use process.env.PASSWORD or a secure secrets manager. Never commit credentials to source control. Add credentials to .gitignore.

rules.title: Secrets Exposed to Client via NEXT_PUBLIC_ Prefix
rules.description: Sensitive credentials exposed to client-side code using NEXT_PUBLIC_ environment variable prefix
rules.recommendation: Remove NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix from sensitive variables. Use server-side environment variables (without NEXT_PUBLIC_) and access them in API routes or getServerSideProps. Only use NEXT_PUBLIC_ for truly public values like API endpoints or publishable keys.

rules.title: PHI Data Access Without Role/Permission Verification
rules.description: Database query accessing PHI data (patient, health, medical, diagnosis, treatment, prescription) without role or permission verification
rules.recommendation: Add role/permission verification before accessing PHI data. Example: if (!hasPermission(user, "read:patients")) throw new Error("Unauthorized"). Implement RBAC middleware to verify user roles before database queries.

rules.title: Service Role Key or Admin Default in Client Code
rules.description: Privileged service_role key exposed in client-side code, isAdmin set to true as default, or conditions that always grant admin access
rules.recommendation: Remove service_role keys from client-side code - these should only exist in server-side API routes. Never default isAdmin to true. Implement proper role assignment based on authenticated user data from secure backend.

rules.title: SELECT * on PHI Tables Violates Minimum Necessary Principle
rules.description: Query uses SELECT * or .select("*") on tables containing PHI, retrieving more data than necessary in violation of HIPAA minimum necessary principle
rules.recommendation: Select only the minimum necessary fields required for the operation. Example: Instead of SELECT * FROM patients, use SELECT id, name, dob FROM patients. For ORMs: .select("id, name, dob") or use field projections.

rules.title: Unsanitized User Input in Database Operations
rules.description: User input from req.body, req.params, or req.query used directly in database operations (insert, update, query, sql, where) without validation (zod, yup, joi, validate, sanitize, parse)
rules.recommendation: Always validate and sanitize user input before database operations. Use validation libraries like Zod, Yup, or Joi. Example: const validated = schema.parse(req.body); await db.insert(validated). Never use raw req.body/params/query directly in database operations.

rules.title: Insecure File Upload Configuration
rules.description: File upload configuration (multer, formidable, busboy) missing fileFilter, limits, or MIME type validation
rules.recommendation: Configure file upload middleware with proper validation. Example: multer({ fileFilter: (req, file, cb) => { if (allowedMimes.includes(file.mimetype)) cb(null, true); else cb(new Error("Invalid file type")); }, limits: { fileSize: 5 * 1024 * 1024 } }). Always validate file type, size, and extension.

rules.title: JWT Without Server-Side Revocation Mechanism
rules.description: JWT token generation (jwt.sign, jsonwebtoken, jose) without server-side revocation mechanism (revoke, blacklist, allowlist, tokenStore, invalidate, denylist). HIPAA NPRM requires ability to revoke access within 1 hour.
rules.recommendation: Implement server-side token revocation mechanism. Store active tokens in Redis/database with TTL, or maintain a blacklist of revoked tokens. Example: await redis.set(`token:${userId}`, token, "EX", 3600); await redis.del(`token:${userId}`) to revoke. HIPAA requires ability to revoke access within 1 hour.

rules.title: Excessive Token Expiration Time
rules.description: JWT token with excessive expiration time. Access tokens should expire within 24 hours (expiresIn: "24h" or less). Refresh tokens should not exceed 7 days for sensitive data.
rules.recommendation: Use short-lived access tokens (≤ 24 hours): expiresIn: "1h" or expiresIn: "15m". For longer sessions, implement refresh token rotation. Example: accessToken with expiresIn: "1h", refreshToken with expiresIn: "7d". Reduce attack window if token is compromised.

rules.title: SELECT * on sensitive table
rules.description: Using SELECT * may retrieve more PHI than necessary.
rules.recommendation: Select only required columns to minimize PHI exposure (minimum necessary).

rules.title: Hardcoded admin role
rules.description: Hardcoded administrative role assignment detected.
rules.recommendation: Use role-based access control (RBAC) with proper authentication.

rules.title: Potential authentication bypass
rules.description: Code pattern suggests authentication may be bypassed.
rules.recommendation: Remove any authentication bypass mechanisms in production code.

rules.title: Hardcoded admin flag
rules.description: Admin privileges set via hardcoded flag.
rules.recommendation: Determine admin status through secure authentication flow.

rules.title: Password field with public visibility
rules.description: Password field may have public accessibility.
rules.recommendation: Password fields should be private and never exposed.

rules.title: CORS wildcard origin
rules.description: CORS configured to allow all origins.
rules.recommendation: Restrict CORS to specific trusted domains for PHI-handling endpoints.

rules.title: Session without expiration
rules.description: Session configured without expiration.
rules.recommendation: Implement automatic session timeout for PHI access (HIPAA recommends 15 min idle).

rules.title: PHI retention period may be too short
rules.description: Data deletion configured with period shorter than HIPAA requirements.
rules.recommendation: HIPAA requires PHI retention for 6 years from creation or last effective date.

rules.title: Data deletion without apparent logging
rules.description: Data deletion operation without visible audit logging.
rules.recommendation: Log all PHI deletions with timestamp, user, and record identifiers.

rules.title: Bulk data deletion operation
rules.description: Bulk deletion (TRUNCATE/DROP) could delete PHI without proper retention.
rules.recommendation: Implement soft-delete with retention periods before permanent deletion.

rules.title: Backup may be disabled
rules.description: Code pattern suggests backups might be disabled.
rules.recommendation: Maintain encrypted backups with proper retention for disaster recovery.

rules.title: PHI caching detected
rules.description: Patient data may be cached, requiring retention policy consideration.
rules.recommendation: Ensure cached PHI has appropriate TTL and is encrypted at rest.

rules.title: Hardcoded password detected
rules.description: A password appears to be hardcoded in the source code.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager for credentials. Never commit passwords to source control.

rules.title: Hardcoded password (pwd) detected
rules.description: A password appears to be hardcoded using "pwd" variable.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager for credentials.

rules.title: Hardcoded secret detected
rules.description: A secret value appears to be hardcoded in the source code.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables or a secrets manager for secrets.

rules.title: Credentials object with password
rules.description: A credentials object containing password field was detected.
rules.recommendation: Load credentials from secure configuration, not source code.

rules.title: API key exposed in source
rules.description: An API key appears to be hardcoded in the source code.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables for API keys. Add to .gitignore and use .env files.

rules.title: API key (apikey) exposed in source
rules.description: An API key appears to be hardcoded.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables for API keys.

rules.title: Stripe API key exposed
rules.description: A Stripe API key pattern was detected in the source code.
rules.recommendation: Never commit Stripe keys. Use environment variables and restrict key permissions.

rules.title: AWS Access Key exposed
rules.description: An AWS Access Key ID pattern was detected.
rules.recommendation: Rotate this key immediately. Use IAM roles or environment variables instead.

rules.title: Bearer token in source
rules.description: A bearer token appears to be hardcoded.
rules.recommendation: Tokens should be fetched at runtime, not hardcoded.

rules.title: Auth token exposed in source
rules.description: An authentication token appears to be hardcoded.
rules.recommendation: Use secure token management. Never commit tokens to source control.

rules.title: Private key exposed in source
rules.description: A private key appears to be embedded in source code.
rules.recommendation: Never commit private keys. Use secure key management services.

rules.title: MongoDB URI with credentials
rules.description: A MongoDB connection string with embedded credentials was detected.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables for database connection strings.

rules.title: PostgreSQL URI with credentials
rules.description: A PostgreSQL connection string with embedded credentials was detected.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables for database connection strings.

rules.title: MySQL URI with credentials
rules.description: A MySQL connection string with embedded credentials was detected.
rules.recommendation: Use environment variables for database connection strings.

rules.title: Unsanitized innerHTML assignment
rules.description: Direct innerHTML assignment without sanitization can lead to XSS vulnerabilities.
rules.recommendation: Use textContent for text, or sanitize HTML with DOMPurify before innerHTML assignment.

rules.title: dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage
rules.description: Using dangerouslySetInnerHTML can expose the application to XSS attacks.
rules.recommendation: Sanitize content with DOMPurify before using dangerouslySetInnerHTML.

rules.title: eval() usage detected
rules.description: Using eval() can execute arbitrary code and is a security risk.
rules.recommendation: Avoid eval(). Use safer alternatives like JSON.parse() for data parsing.

rules.title: Function constructor usage
rules.description: The Function constructor can execute arbitrary code like eval().
rules.recommendation: Avoid dynamic code execution. Use predefined functions instead.

rules.title: document.write usage
rules.description: document.write can be exploited for XSS and blocks page rendering.
rules.recommendation: Use DOM manipulation methods (appendChild, insertAdjacentHTML) instead.

rules.title: SQL query string concatenation
rules.description: Building SQL queries with string concatenation is vulnerable to SQL injection.
rules.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements. Never concatenate user input into SQL.

rules.title: SQL query with template literal interpolation
rules.description: Interpolating variables directly into SQL queries enables SQL injection.
rules.recommendation: Use parameterized queries. Pass variables as parameters, not interpolated strings.

rules.title: Database query with template interpolation
rules.description: Template literal interpolation in database queries can lead to injection attacks.
rules.recommendation: Use parameterized queries: query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1", [userId])

rules.title: SQL execute with string concatenation
rules.description: Concatenating strings in SQL execute statements enables injection.
rules.recommendation: Use parameterized queries instead of string concatenation.

rules.title: Raw SQL query with interpolation
rules.description: Raw SQL queries with interpolated values are vulnerable to injection.
rules.recommendation: Even with raw queries, use parameter binding for user-supplied values.

rules.title: Cookie without httpOnly flag
rules.description: Cookies without httpOnly can be accessed by JavaScript, enabling XSS attacks.
rules.recommendation: Add httpOnly: true and secure: true to all cookies, especially session cookies.

rules.title: Cookie configuration missing security flags
rules.description: Cookie configuration is missing httpOnly and/or secure flags.
rules.recommendation: Always set httpOnly: true, secure: true for cookies handling sensitive data.

rules.title: No audit logging framework detected
rules.description: No recognized logging framework found in dependencies.
rules.recommendation: Implement structured audit logging using winston, pino, or similar.

rules.title: PHI operation may lack audit logging
rules.description: A create, update, delete, read, or auth operation on PHI-related data was found without apparent audit logging in the same file.
rules.recommendation: Log all PHI operations with timestamp, user ID, and action details.

rules.title: MD5 hash function
rules.description: MD5 hash function is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: SHA1 hash function
rules.description: SHA1 hash function is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: DES encryption
rules.description: DES encryption is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: RC4 encryption
rules.description: RC4 encryption is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: Deprecated cipher method
rules.description: Deprecated cipher method is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: ECB mode encryption
rules.description: ECB mode encryption is not suitable for protecting PHI.
rules.recommendation: Use AES-256-GCM for encryption and SHA-256 or stronger for hashing.

rules.title: Unencrypted HTTP URL
rules.description: Unencrypted HTTP URL may expose PHI in transit or at rest.
rules.recommendation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for PHI in transit and encrypt PHI (including backups) at rest.

rules.title: SSL disabled
rules.description: SSL disabled may expose PHI in transit or at rest.
rules.recommendation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for PHI in transit and encrypt PHI (including backups) at rest.

rules.title: SSL verification disabled
rules.description: SSL verification disabled may expose PHI in transit or at rest.
rules.recommendation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for PHI in transit and encrypt PHI (including backups) at rest.
